message:
install: Add proper fish shell PATH support

The add_to_path() function now correctly handles fish shell's PATH syntax:

CHANGES:
  - Detect shell type via $SHELL basename before generating PATH line
  - Fish shell: use 'fish_add_path <dir>' instead of export syntax
  - POSIX shells (bash/zsh): continue using 'export PATH="<dir>:$PATH"'
  - Create config directory if it doesn't exist (fish stores config in
    ~/.config/fish/config.fish which may not exist on fresh installs)

The previous implementation would write POSIX-style export statements to
fish config files, which fish cannot parse. This caused silent failures
where PATH modifications appeared to succeed but had no effect.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -72,7 +72,7 @@ get_shell_config() {
 add_to_path() {
     local install_dir="$1"
     local shell_config="$2"
-    local path_line="export PATH=\"${install_dir}:\$PATH\""
+    local shell_name=$(basename "${SHELL:-/bin/bash}")
 
     # Check if already in PATH
     if [[ ":$PATH:" == *":${install_dir}:"* ]]; then
@@ -86,6 +86,20 @@ add_to_path() {
         return 0
     fi
 
+    # Ensure config directory exists (important for fish)
+    local config_dir=$(dirname "$shell_config")
+    if [[ ! -d "$config_dir" ]]; then
+        mkdir -p "$config_dir"
+    fi
+
+    # Generate shell-appropriate PATH addition
+    local path_line
+    if [[ "$shell_name" == "fish" ]]; then
+        path_line="fish_add_path ${install_dir}"
+    else
+        path_line="export PATH=\"${install_dir}:\$PATH\""
+    fi
+
     # Add to config
     echo "" >> "$shell_config"
     echo "# Added by giil installer" >> "$shell_config"
